test_description: This test case evaluates the generator model's accuracy and technical depth, assuming a perfect retrieval process.
question: [Fill] In heterogeneous 3D monolithic CFETs, the top-tier p-FETs employ a Ge (_____) /<110> gate-all-around nanosheet channel to achieve record-high hole mobility, while the bottom-tier n-FETs utilize a Si (_____) /<110> tri-gate structure, enabling mobility matching that reduces power consumption and improves area efficiency.

gold_context_sources.section_title: Ge(110) GAA Nanosheet / Si(100) Tri-gate Nanosheet Monolithic CFETs Featuring Record-High Hole Mobility
gold_context_sources.evidence: We demonstrated heterogeneous 3D monolithic CFETs (mCFETs) by utilizing Ge (110)/<110> gate-all-around (GAA) nanosheet p-FETs as the top-tier transistors and Si (100)/<110> tri-gate n-FETs as the bottom-tier transistors. By minimizing the mobility difference between electrons and holes through this transport combination of heterogeneous channels, we demonstrated its potential for the next-generation logic.

gold_context_sources.section_title: Ge(110) GAA Nanosheet / Si(100) Tri-gate Nanosheet Monolithic CFETs Featuring Record-High Hole Mobility
gold_context_sources.evidence: We first demonstrated Ge (110) GAA p-FETs for reducing mobility mismatch between electron and hole in the mCFETs. As a result, we have achieved a remarkable μeff of 440 cm2/V∙s (1200 cm2/V∙s normalized by footprint), the highest reported so far for Ge p-FETs at room temperature. By integrating this advanced channel on the Si bottom-tier n-FETs, we successfully demonstrated heterogeneous mCFETs, highlighting inverter operation.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [110] for the Ge top-tier p-FETs and [100] for the Si bottom-tier n-FETs. Reasoning: The heterogeneous 3D monolithic CFET architecture employs Ge (110)/<110> gate-all-around nanosheet channels for the top-tier p-FETs, achieving record-high hole mobility (440 cm2/V∙s effective mobility, normalized 1200 cm2/V∙s) by leveraging the anisotropic valence band and high-quality epitaxial growth. The bottom-tier n-FETs use Si (100)/<110> tri-gate nanosheet channels, forming a complementary pairing that minimizes electron-hole mobility mismatch. This combination reduces power consumption by enabling lower supply voltages and improves area efficiency by allowing reduced channel widths, as experimentally demonstrated in the referenced study.
